visa_types.overview:
Overview
For  Adult Applicants (18 Years old and above)
The Passport Renewal Centre can assist any applicants  wishing to renew their ePassports as long as there are no changes on the  details of the biographical page.  Passports  issued to adults will be valid for 10 years.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For  Adult Applicants (18 Years old and above)
The Passport Renewal Centre can assist any applicants  wishing to renew their ePassports as long as there are no changes on the  details of the biographical page.  Passports  issued to adults will be valid for 10 years.

## Application Fees

[Table:  Adults]
Please Note:
- Application fee for renewal of passport is payable in cash or credit/debit card. The passport renewal fee is the same as the fee charged at the Philippine Consulate General for the renewal of Philippine passports.
- Applicant shall be charged a convenience fee of KWD 10.500  per application in addition to the other fees and must be paid in cash or card (all applicable taxes included). The applicable fee in KWD is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Personal appearance of the applicant at the Passport Renewal Centre is required
- Accomplished Online Application Form. 
click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
- Original Passport
- Photocopy of current Passport and visa.

## Photo Specifications
Passport photograph  will be captured at the Passport  Renewal Centre.
Important Reminders:
Eyeglasses, contact lenses, jewelry  (earring, necklace), heavy make-up    should  be removed before  photo capturing.   It is also advisable for applicants having sore eyes, eye sty or any redness in  the eyes to come for e-passport renewal when their eye condition improves

## Processing Time
The new passport may be released within 3-6 weeks from the date of submission of application, barring unforeseen events

## Application Form
To complete your online application form
click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)

visa_types.name: Passport Services
visa_types.overview:
Welcome to the website of the Philippine Passport Renewal.Center |(PaRC)
In an effort to ease the passport renewal process for Philippine national overseas, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) and VFS Global signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) on the establishment of a Passport Renewal Center (PaRC).
PaRC aims to provide an alternative means for Filipinos to avail of passport renewal services in the Middle East Countries with the highest concentration of Filipino workers.
PaRC is authorized by the DFA to accept passport applications from Filipinos holding electronic passports and whose applications do not entail a change in passport record of the holder. While PaRC is a stand-alone facility, its operation is supervised by the Philippine Consulate General.. This means that the Consulate monitors passport applications lodged through PaRC and the passports that are produced go through the Consulate.
This site contains important information on passport renewal process at PaRC. Please go through the contents carefully before proceeding with your application.
visa_types.full_text:
Welcome to the website of the Philippine Passport Renewal.Center |(PaRC)
In an effort to ease the passport renewal process for Philippine national overseas, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) and VFS Global signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) on the establishment of a Passport Renewal Center (PaRC).
PaRC aims to provide an alternative means for Filipinos to avail of passport renewal services in the Middle East Countries with the highest concentration of Filipino workers.
PaRC is authorized by the DFA to accept passport applications from Filipinos holding electronic passports and whose applications do not entail a change in passport record of the holder. While PaRC is a stand-alone facility, its operation is supervised by the Philippine Consulate General.. This means that the Consulate monitors passport applications lodged through PaRC and the passports that are produced go through the Consulate.
This site contains important information on passport renewal process at PaRC. Please go through the contents carefully before proceeding with your application.

visa_types.name: Minor
visa_types.documents_required:
- Personal appearance of the minor applicant at the ePassport Renewal Centre is a must. Minor must be accompanied by either parent.
- Accomplished Online Application Form.
click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
- Original Passport
- Photocopy of current Passport and visa.
- Copy of Birth Certificate (PSA or MOH)
- Photocopy of passport of both Parents
- Marriage contract of parents, if available.
Important Instruction for applicant:
Personal appearance of the child and either parent. If the child’s age is seven (7) years and below, only the mother can apply for the child’s passport;
Certificate of Marriage/Report of Marriage issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) duly authenticated by the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) in Manila is required if only the father files the application. (Original and photocopy)
Certificate of Live Birth/Report of Birth issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA). (Original and photocopy)
Passport of either parent with one (1) photocopy of data page
visa_types.processing_time: The new passport may be released within 4-6 weeks from the date of submission of application, barring unforeseen events.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Minor Applicants (below 18 years old)
Passport to be issued to a minor child will be valid for 5 years.
A minor applicant must be accompanied by either parent during the time of application.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Minor Applicants (below 18 years old)
Passport to be issued to a minor child will be valid for 5 years.
A minor applicant must be accompanied by either parent during the time of application.

## Application Fees

[Table:  Minor]
Please Note:
- Application       fee for renewal of passport is payable in cash or debit/credit card.  The passport renewal fee is similar to       the fee charged at the Philippine Consulate General for the renewal of       Philippine passports.
- Applicant shall be charged a convenience fee of KWF 10.500  per application in addition to the other fees and must be paid in  cash or card (all applicable taxes included). The applicable fee in KWD is as per the current exchange rate. It is  subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.

## Documents Required
- Personal appearance of the minor applicant at the ePassport Renewal Centre is a must. Minor must be accompanied by either parent.
- Accomplished Online Application Form.
click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
- Original Passport
- Photocopy of current Passport and visa.
- Copy of Birth Certificate (PSA or MOH)
- Photocopy of passport of both Parents
- Marriage contract of parents, if available.
Important Instruction for applicant:
Personal appearance of the child and either parent. If the child’s age is seven (7) years and below, only the mother can apply for the child’s passport;
Certificate of Marriage/Report of Marriage issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) duly authenticated by the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) in Manila is required if only the father files the application. (Original and photocopy)
Certificate of Live Birth/Report of Birth issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA). (Original and photocopy)
Passport of either parent with one (1) photocopy of data page

## Photo Specifications
Passport photograph will be captured at the Passport Renewal  Centre.
Important Reminders:
Eyeglasses, contact  lenses, jewelry (earring, necklace), heavy make-up should be removed before  photo capturing.  It is also advisable for applicants having sore eyes,  eye sty or any redness in the eyes to come for e-passport renewal when their  eye condition improves

## Processing Time
The new passport may be released within 4-6 weeks from the date of submission of application, barring unforeseen events.

## Application Form
To complete your online application form
click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
